# Academic Personal Homepage — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build and deploy a complete academic personal homepage at `https://tienenCUHK.github.io` using Jekyll + al-folio theme.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Jekyll with the al-folio academic theme. Content authored in Markdown + BibTeX + YAML config. GitHub Actions builds the site and deploys to the `gh-pages` branch on every push to `main`.

**Tech Stack:** Ruby 2.6+, Jekyll 4.x, al-folio theme, GitHub Pages (via GitHub Actions)

## Global Constraints

- Repo: `tienenCUHK/tienenCUHK.github.io` (already exists on GitHub)
- Theme: al-folio (minimalist academic style)
- Style: white background, deep blue `#003366` links, serif headings, 800px max-width
- Navigation: Home · Publications · Projects · Blog · CV
- Dark mode: auto (OS preference)
- Deployment: push to `main` → GitHub Actions → `gh-pages` branch

---

## File Structure Map

```
.
├── _config.yml                 # Site config (name, affiliation, socials, theme settings)
├── _bibliography/
│   └── papers.bib              # BibTeX export from Google Scholar / DBLP
├── _pages/
│   ├── about.md                # Home page: bio, photo, interests, news
│   ├── publications.md         # Publications page (auto-generated from .bib)
│   ├── projects.md             # Projects page
│   ├── cv.md                   # CV page (PDF embed + text)
│   └── blog.md                 # Blog listing page
├── _posts/                     # Blog posts (YYYY-MM-DD-title.md)
│   └── 2026-01-01-welcome.md   # Starter post
├── _projects/                  # Individual project pages
│   └── example-project.md
├── _news/                      # News items for homepage
│   └── welcome.md
├── _data/
│   └── coauthors.yml           # Co-author data (auto-links in publications)
├── assets/
│   ├── img/
│   │   └── prof_pic.jpg        # Profile photo (~400x400px)
│   └── pdf/
│       └── cv.pdf              # CV PDF download
├── _layouts/                   # (Theme defaults, customize only if needed)
├── _sass/                      # CSS overrides
├── Gemfile                     # Ruby dependencies
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── deploy.yml          # GitHub Actions deploy workflow
└── README.md
```

---

### Task 1: Install Ruby Environment & Jekyll

**Files:**
- Modify: `Gemfile`

**What this does:** Set up the local development environment so Jekyll can build and preview the site.

- [ ] **Step 1: Install Bundler and Jekyll gems**

Run:
```bash
gem install bundler jekyll --user-install
```
Expected: both gems install successfully.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create Gemfile**

Write `Gemfile`:

```ruby
source "https://rubygems.org"

gem "jekyll", "~> 4.3"
gem "jekyll-paginate-v2"
gem "jekyll-sitemap"
gem "jekyll-scholar"
gem "jekyll-feed"
gem "jekyll-seo-tag"
gem "jekyll-email-protect"
gem "jekyll-minifier"
gem "jekyll-target-blank"
gem "unicode_utils"
gem "webrick"
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Install dependencies**

Run:
```bash
bundle install
```
Expected: all gems install without errors.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify Jekyll works**

Run:
```bash
bundle exec jekyll --version
```
Expected: prints Jekyll version (4.x).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add Gemfile Gemfile.lock
git commit -m "chore: add Gemfile with Jekyll dependencies"
```

---

### Task 2: Scaffold al-folio Theme & Directory Structure

**Files:**
- Create: `_config.yml`, `_layouts/`, `_includes/`, `_sass/`, `assets/`, `_data/`
- Create: Directory structure per File Structure Map above

**What this does:** Pull the al-folio theme files and create the full directory skeleton.

- [ ] **Step 1: Clone al-folio theme into a temporary location**

Run:
```bash
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/alshedivat/al-folio.git /tmp/al-folio-temp
```
Expected: repo cloned to `/tmp/al-folio-temp`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Copy theme files into project**

Copy the essential theme directories and files (NOT overwriting our Gemfile):

```bash
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_config.yml .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/_layouts .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/_includes .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/_sass .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/assets .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/_data .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/_plugins .
cp -r /tmp/al-folio-temp/.github .
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/404.html .
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/CNAME 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/LICENSE .
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/robots.txt .
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/*.js 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/package.json 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_news . -r 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_bibliography . -r 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_pages . -r 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_posts . -r 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/_projects . -r 2>/dev/null || true
cp /tmp/al-folio-temp/blog . -r 2>/dev/null || true
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create content directory structure**

Run:
```bash
mkdir -p _news _bibliography _pages _posts _projects assets/img assets/pdf
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Clean up temp**

```bash
rm -rf /tmp/al-folio-temp
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: scaffold al-folio theme structure"
```

---

### Task 3: Configure Site Identity (`_config.yml`)

**Files:**
- Modify: `_config.yml`

**What this does:** Set the site's core identity — name, affiliation, social links, theme appearance.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update personal info in _config.yml**

Edit `_config.yml` — set these key values:

```yaml
title: "Your Name"                          # Full name
email: "yourname@cuhk.edu.hk"               # Academic email
description: "PhD Student in AI for Science at CUHK"
url: "https://tienenCUHK.github.io"
baseurl: ""

# Social / scholar links (fill in your actual handles):
github_username: tienenCUHK
scholar_userid: ""                          # Google Scholar ID (from your scholar URL)
orcid_id: ""                                # ORCID ID
twitter_username: ""                        # Optional
linkedin_username: ""                       # Optional

# Theme appearance
repository: "tienenCUHK/tienenCUHK.github.io"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Configure theme color**

In `_config.yml`, set the theme color variable:
```yaml
accent_color: "#003366"
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Set navigation menu**

In `_config.yml`, ensure nav pages are listed:
```yaml
navbar_items:
  - title: "Home"
    url: "/"
  - title: "Publications"
    url: "/publications/"
  - title: "Projects"
    url: "/projects/"
  - title: "Blog"
    url: "/blog/"
  - title: "CV"
    url: "/cv/"
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add _config.yml
git commit -m "feat: configure site identity and navigation"
```

---

### Task 4: Set Up Home Page (About)

**Files:**
- Create: `_pages/about.md`
- Create: `_news/welcome.md`
- Place: `assets/img/prof_pic.jpg` (user provides photo)

**What this does:** Create the landing page with bio, research interests, contact info, and news.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the About page**

Write `_pages/about.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: about
title: About
permalink: /
subtitle: PhD Student in AI for Science · <a href='https://www.cuhk.edu.hk'>CUHK</a>

profile:
  align: right
  image: prof_pic.jpg
  image_circular: true
  more_info: >
    <p>Office: [Your Office]</p>
    <p>[Your Department]</p>
    <p>The Chinese University of Hong Kong</p>

news: true
selected_papers: true
social: true
---

I am a PhD student at [Department], The Chinese University of Hong Kong,
advised by [Advisor Name]. My research lies at the intersection of
**artificial intelligence** and **scientific discovery** —
building machine learning methods for [your specific domain].

**Research Interests:**
- AI for Scientific Discovery
- [Interest 2]
- [Interest 3]
- [Interest 4]
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write a starter news item**

Write `_news/welcome.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: post
date: 2026-07-19
inline: true
related_posts: false
---

Website launched! 🎉 Check out my [publications](/publications/) and [projects](/projects/).
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add a placeholder profile photo**

Run:
```bash
# Until the user provides their real photo, use a placeholder:
curl -sL https://via.placeholder.com/400x400/003366/ffffff?text=Photo -o assets/img/prof_pic.jpg
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add _pages/about.md _news/welcome.md assets/img/prof_pic.jpg
git commit -m "feat: add home page with bio, news, and profile photo"
```

---

### Task 5: Set Up Publications Page

**Files:**
- Create: `_pages/publications.md`
- Create: `_bibliography/papers.bib`

**What this does:** Create a publications listing driven by a BibTeX file. Papers display with year grouping, venue badges, BibTeX citation popups, and PDF links.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the Publications page**

Write `_pages/publications.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /publications/
title: Publications
description: Publications by categories in reversed chronological order.
nav: true
nav_order: 2
years: [2026, 2025, 2024, 2023]
---

<!-- _pages/publications.md -->

<div class="publications">

{% bibliography --group_by year --group_order descending %}

</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create a starter BibTeX file**

Write `_bibliography/papers.bib`:

```bibtex
@article{example2026,
  title={Example Paper: AI for Scientific Discovery},
  author={Your Name and Co-author},
  journal={arXiv preprint},
  year={2026},
  note={Under review},
  selected={true},
  preview={example.png},
  url={https://arxiv.org/abs/xxxx},
  html={https://arxiv.org/abs/xxxx},
  pdf={example.pdf}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify Jekyll builds without BibTeX errors**

Run:
```bash
bundle exec jekyll build
```
Expected: build succeeds (some warnings about missing fields in .bib are OK).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add _pages/publications.md _bibliography/papers.bib
git commit -m "feat: add publications page with BibTeX example"
```

---

### Task 6: Set Up Projects Page

**Files:**
- Create: `_pages/projects.md`
- Create: `_projects/example-project.md`

**What this does:** Create a projects listing page with card-based layout for research projects.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the Projects listing page**

Write `_pages/projects.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: page
title: Projects
permalink: /projects/
description: Research projects I'm working on.
nav: true
nav_order: 3
display_categories: [research]
horizontal: false
---

<!-- _pages/projects.md -->

<div class="projects">
  {% if site.enable_project_categories and page.display_categories %}
    {% for category in page.display_categories %}
      <h2 class="category">{{ category }}</h2>
      {% assign categorized_projects = site.projects | where: "category", category %}
      {% assign sorted_projects = categorized_projects | sort: "importance" %}
      {% include projects.liquid %}
    {% endfor %}
  {% else %}
    {% assign sorted_projects = site.projects | sort: "importance" %}
    {% include projects.liquid %}
  {% endif %}
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write an example project**

Write `_projects/example-project.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: page
title: Example Research Project
description: >
  A short description of this research project — what problem it solves
  and the key idea.
img: assets/img/projects/project_placeholder.jpg
importance: 1
category: research
related_publications: example2026
---

## Overview

Describe your project here. Explain the motivation, approach, and key results.

**Key contributions:**
- Contribution 1
- Contribution 2

**Links:** [Paper](#) · [Code](https://github.com/tienenCUHK) · [Demo](#)
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add a project placeholder image**

```bash
mkdir -p assets/img/projects
curl -sL https://via.placeholder.com/800x400/003366/ffffff?text=Project -o assets/img/projects/project_placeholder.jpg
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add _pages/projects.md _projects/ assets/img/projects/
git commit -m "feat: add projects page with example project"
```

### Task 7: Set Up Blog

**Files:**
- Create: `_pages/blog.md` or use theme default
- Create: `_posts/2026-07-19-welcome.md`

**What this does:** Set up blog functionality — post listing with year archive on the sidebar, individual posts in Markdown.

- [ ] **Step 1: Ensure Blog listing page exists**

Check if the theme already includes a blog index page at `_pages/blog.md` or `blog/index.html`. If not, create `_pages/blog.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /blog/
title: Blog
description: Notes, tutorials, and thoughts on AI for Science.
nav: true
nav_order: 4
---
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write a welcome blog post**

Write `_posts/2026-07-19-welcome.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: post
title: "Welcome to My Blog"
date: 2026-07-19
tags: [meta]
categories: [blog]
---

This is my academic blog where I share notes on AI for Science, paper
summaries, tutorials, and thoughts on research.

Stay tuned for more posts!
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify blog post renders**

Run:
```bash
bundle exec jekyll build
ls _site/blog/
```
Expected: `_site/blog/` exists with generated HTML.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add _pages/blog.md _posts/2026-07-19-welcome.md 2>/dev/null
git add blog/ 2>/dev/null || true
git commit -m "feat: set up blog with welcome post"
```

### Task 8: Set Up CV Page

**Files:**
- Create: `_pages/cv.md`
- Place: `assets/pdf/cv.pdf` (user provides their CV)

**What this does:** Create a CV page with an embedded PDF viewer and download link.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the CV page**

Write `_pages/cv.md`:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /cv/
title: CV
description: My curriculum vitae.
nav: true
nav_order: 5
---

<div class="cv">
  <a href="/assets/pdf/cv.pdf" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <i class="fas fa-file-pdf"></i> Download CV (PDF)
  </a>

  <iframe src="/assets/pdf/cv.pdf" width="100%" height="800px" style="margin-top: 1em; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">
  </iframe>
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add a placeholder CV PDF**

Since the user may not have a PDF ready yet, create a placeholder:

```bash
echo "CV coming soon." > assets/pdf/cv.md
# PDF will be placed by the user: assets/pdf/cv.pdf
```

For now, adjust the CV page to not break without a PDF:

```markdown
---
layout: page
permalink: /cv/
title: CV
description: My curriculum vitae.
nav: true
nav_order: 5
---

{% if site.cv_pdf %}
  <a href="{{ site.cv_pdf | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' }}" class="btn btn-sm z-depth-0" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <i class="fas fa-file-pdf"></i> Download CV (PDF)
  </a>

  <iframe src="{{ site.cv_pdf | prepend: '/assets/pdf/' }}" width="100%" height="800px" style="margin-top: 1em; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">
  </iframe>
{% else %}
  <p>📄 CV will be uploaded soon. Please check back later or <a href="mailto:{{ site.email }}">email me</a>.</p>
{% endif %}
```

And in `_config.yml`, add:
```yaml
cv_pdf: ""   # Set to "cv.pdf" once you place your CV at assets/pdf/cv.pdf
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add _pages/cv.md
git commit -m "feat: add CV page with PDF embed support"
```

---

### Task 9: Configure GitHub Actions Deployment

**Files:**
- Create/Modify: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

**What this does:** Set up the CI/CD pipeline so every push to `main` builds the site and deploys to `gh-pages` branch.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the GitHub Actions deploy workflow**

Write `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:

```yaml
name: Deploy to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: "pages"
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Ruby
        uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: '3.2'
          bundler-cache: true

      - name: Build site
        run: bundle exec jekyll build
        env:
          JEKYLL_ENV: production

      - name: Deploy to gh-pages
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./_site
          publish_branch: gh-pages
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify workflow syntax**

Confirm the YAML is valid and matches the structure of `.github/workflows/` files from the theme.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/deploy.yml
git commit -m "ci: add GitHub Actions deploy workflow"
```

---

### Task 10: Build Locally, Push, and Deploy

**Files:**
- All files from previous tasks

**What this does:** Final build verification, push to GitHub, and confirm deployment.

- [ ] **Step 1: Full local build test**

Run:
```bash
bundle exec jekyll build
```
Expected: site builds to `_site/` with no fatal errors.

- [ ] **Step 2: Start local preview server and check**

Run:
```bash
bundle exec jekyll serve --livereload
```
Open `http://localhost:4000` in browser. Verify:
- Home page shows bio, photo, news
- Publications page lists the example paper
- Projects page shows the example project
- Blog page shows the welcome post
- CV page shows the "coming soon" message
- Navigation works across all pages

- [ ] **Step 3: Add _site to .gitignore and push**

Ensure `.gitignore` includes:
```
_site
.sass-cache
.jekyll-cache
.jekyll-metadata
vendor
```

Then push:
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: finalize site, add .gitignore"
git push origin main
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Configure GitHub Pages in repo settings**

After push, go to GitHub repo → Settings → Pages:
- Source: "Deploy from a branch"
- Branch: `gh-pages` → `/ (root)`
- Save

- [ ] **Step 5: Wait for deploy and verify**

Check the Actions tab for the deploy workflow. Once green, visit:
`https://tienenCUHK.github.io`

---

### Task 11: Post-Deployment — Replace Placeholders with Real Content

**Files:**
- Replace: `assets/img/prof_pic.jpg` (real photo)
- Replace: `_bibliography/papers.bib` (real publications)
- Replace: `assets/pdf/cv.pdf` (real CV)
- Modify: `_config.yml` (real name, email, social IDs)
- Modify: `_pages/about.md` (real bio, interests)
- Modify: `_projects/` (real project pages)

**What this does:** Replace all placeholder content with the user's actual academic information.

- [ ] **Step 1: Swap profile photo**

Replace `assets/img/prof_pic.jpg` with your real photo (400×400px square recommended).

- [ ] **Step 2: Update personal info**

In `_config.yml`, fill in your actual:
- Name, email, affiliation
- Google Scholar user ID
- ORCID ID
- Twitter/LinkedIn (if desired)

- [ ] **Step 3: Import real publications**

Export your BibTeX from [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com) or [DBLP](https://dblp.org):
- Go to your Google Scholar profile
- Select all publications → Export → BibTeX
- Copy into `_bibliography/papers.bib`, replacing the example entry

- [ ] **Step 4: Upload real CV**

Place your CV PDF at `assets/pdf/cv.pdf`.
In `_config.yml`, set `cv_pdf: "cv.pdf"`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Update About page**

Edit `_pages/about.md` with your real bio, research interests, department, and advisor.

- [ ] **Step 6: Add real projects**

For each research project, create a new `_projects/<project-slug>.md` file following the example pattern. Add real screenshots to `assets/img/projects/`.

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit and push**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "content: replace placeholders with real academic information"
git push origin main
```

Verify your live site at `https://tienenCUHK.github.io`.
